name: AXS Delayed Retest Continuation v1
thesis: AXS 1h is the structurally different lane for testing delayed retest continuation instead of immediate breakout chasing. The edge should come from pullback acceptance after an initial break, with management designed to monetize the second leg rather than the first impulse.
invalidation_condition: Abort if retests are too rare, if the retest qualifier and continuation filters conflict, or if second-leg follow-through is too weak after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A breakout occurs, price retests rather than extending immediately, and continuation resumes once the retest holds.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Many failed breakout ideas enter too early; delaying until retest acceptance can improve participation quality and reduce whipsaw risk in transition regimes.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: AXS 1h is a useful structurally different lane because it should show more obvious pullback-and-resume behavior than the majors while remaining inside the supported basket.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If AXS trades as pure noise rather than structured retests, the continuation leg may never materialize after entry.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries occur but continuation fades too quickly, tighten the time-based de-risk before making retest depth stricter.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; retest hold and DI confirmation are aligned
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: kept reasonable by broad retest-distance requirements

long: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 3 bars if unrealized gain < 0.2 * ATR_14
